response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with strong testing culture and some high-throughput systems exposure, but falls well short of the core requirements for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience; the resume shows roughly six months of professional C/C++ work (National Instruments) plus a thesis project. Career focus is web/full-stack development in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than cloud compute platforms, and there is no formal technical-leadership experience mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers at scale.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and teaching/workshop experience
Broad, long-tenured engineering career spanning web, embedded, DevOps tooling, and open-source project ownership
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ requirement — only ~6 months of professional C/C++ work
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed infrastructure ownership; experience is mostly product/full-stack web
No formal technical-lead role leading strategy, priorities, and cross-team alignment as required
